acceptance_targets: [ch03-booker-001] Pass if the revised sequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:315-406 has three distinct beats where each introduces new consequence (not just new metrics), and no two consecutive notebook-entry blocks exceed 4 lines without an intervening external event; fail otherwise.

[ch03-craft-001] Pass if, in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:237-277, there is at least one new exchange where both Ravi and Anika each make a concrete consequential move (action or decision) tied to the conflict; fail if the scene transitions directly from argument to logistics without that consequence beat.

[ch03-dialogue-001] Pass if, in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:193-237, Anika delivers at least 3 lines anchored to concrete objects/timing/logistics under pressure, Ravi avoids abstract labels like "grief work/avoidance" in favor of procedural language, and the two voices are distinguishable by syntax/rhythm when read aloud; fail otherwise.

[ch03-prose-001] Pass if no more than one binary-negation formula remains across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:81, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:211, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:349, and each replacement sentence introduces a specific sensory or behavioral cue; fail if two or more "not X, not Y" style formulas remain.

[ch03-prose-002] Pass if each late-scene span at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:329-389 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:399-434 contains at most one explicit counting action and at least two non-numeric interior regulation cues that affect behavior; fail if counting remains the dominant repeated mechanism in both spans.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised sequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:315-406 has three distinct beats where each introduces new consequence (not just new metrics), and no two consecutive notebook-entry blocks exceed 4 lines without an intervening external event; fail otherwise.
findings.problem: The final movement repeats notebook-control beats three times with minimal leverage change, so causal momentum into chapter 4 softens instead of escalating.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the sequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:315-329, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:373-390, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:399-406 so each beat changes stakes (e.g., log entry -> external interruption/risk -> binding pre-training decision). Keep one full log block and convert at least one later block into scene action.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:237-277, there is at least one new exchange where both Ravi and Anika each make a concrete consequential move (action or decision) tied to the conflict; fail if the scene transitions directly from argument to logistics without that consequence beat.
findings.problem: Timidity: the confrontation peaks, then quickly retreats into logistics and routine meal/training talk before embodied fallout lands on-page.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:237-277, add an immediate aftermath beat that forces physical/emotional consequence before the routine reset (examples: near-discovery of the missing watch, visible dysregulation, or a sharper boundary exchange that costs one of them something concrete).

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:193-237, Anika delivers at least 3 lines anchored to concrete objects/timing/logistics under pressure, Ravi avoids abstract labels like "grief work/avoidance" in favor of procedural language, and the two voices are distinguishable by syntax/rhythm when read aloud; fail otherwise.
findings.problem: In the key fight, both voices drift into abstract therapeutic framing ("grief work", "avoidance") and polished rhetoric, reducing idiolect separation and pulling Anika away from her concrete/logistics-heavy stress voice in the style bible.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:193-237 with more pressure-native cadence: interruptions, contractions, object/time-specific language, and Ravi's work/schedule lexicon instead of imported therapy terms. Keep the conflict content but localize it to this room, these boxes, this pickup deadline.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if no more than one binary-negation formula remains across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:81, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:211, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:349, and each replacement sentence introduces a specific sensory or behavioral cue; fail if two or more "not X, not Y" style formulas remain.
findings.problem: A recurring binary-negation construction ("not X, not Y" / "not X and not Y") appears more than twice, flattening sentence rhythm into a detectable tic.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the constructions at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:81, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:211, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:349 so at least two are replaced by concrete physical detail or action-based contrast rather than paired negations.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if each late-scene span at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:329-389 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:399-434 contains at most one explicit counting action and at least two non-numeric interior regulation cues that affect behavior; fail if counting remains the dominant repeated mechanism in both spans.
findings.problem: Anika's counting stress-tell is used so frequently that it starts to read as mechanical token reuse rather than evolving interior process.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:329-389 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:399-434, keep counting in one strategic moment per scene, but shift other regulation beats to alternate modalities already established for Anika (touch/sound/body memory/self-assessment) so the coping texture varies.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when it changes behavior: pain detail, debt arithmetic, manipulative care, and mixed motives. In edge cases, prefer the truest in-character action over protective narration, then carry the real consequence forward instead of immediately moralizing it away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If coercion, grief, injury, or economic shame appears, language must stay specific and embodied; vague softening triggers mandatory rewrite.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms are reserved for formal authority, deliberate emphasis, or emotional freezing.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary scene count and dialogue density chapter to chapter: rotate match compression, slow inventory aftermath, institutional pressure scenes, and quiet domestic intervals so adjacent chapters do not share the same structural pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete contemporary diction with class-aware specificity; avoid abstract therapy language unless a character would actually use it.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third anchored in Anika for most chapters, with brief controlled slips toward Ravi in designated confrontation and aftermath chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate clipped athletic sequences with longer reflective cadences; sentence length should track exertion, shame, and release.
